problem_statement.title: Keep quantile levels when modifying a model spec
problem_statement.body:
Closes #1304

Updated the model spec constructor to include the quantile levels (if any) when modifying the spec.
problem_statement.issue_body:
``` r
library(tidymodels)

qnt_lvls <- (1:3) / 4

linear_reg() |> 
  set_engine("quantreg") |> 
  set_mode("quantile regression", quantile_levels = qnt_lvls)
#> Linear Regression Model Specification (quantile regression)
#> 
#> Computational engine: quantreg
#> Quantile levels: 0.25, 0.5, and 0.75.

linear_reg() |> 
  set_mode("quantile regression", quantile_levels = qnt_lvls) |> 
  set_engine("quantreg")
#> Linear Regression Model Specification (quantile regression)
#> 
#> Computational engine: quantreg
#> Quantile levels: .
```

<sup>Created on 2025-11-14 with [reprex v2.1.1](https://reprex.tidyverse.org)</sup>

We should create a constructor function for model specifications to avoid this issue in the future.

diff --git a/DESCRIPTION b/DESCRIPTION
index 286951677..2380f5f6b 100644
--- a/DESCRIPTION
+++ b/DESCRIPTION
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 Package: parsnip
 Title: A Common API to Modeling and Analysis Functions
-Version: 1.4.1.9001
+Version: 1.4.1.9002
 Authors@R: c(
     person("Max", "Kuhn", , "max@posit.co", role = c("cre", "aut"),
            comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0003-2402-136X")),
diff --git a/NEWS.md b/NEWS.md
index d383c2521..3def0085e 100644
--- a/NEWS.md
+++ b/NEWS.md
@@ -1,5 +1,6 @@
 # parsnip (development version)
 
+* Quantile levels are not dropped when a model specification is modified (#1304)
 
 * xgboost and qrnn engines were added for quantile regression to `boost_tree()` and `mlp()`, respectively. (#1321)
 
diff --git a/R/arguments.R b/R/arguments.R
index df67ac8dd..0518a0d18 100644
--- a/R/arguments.R
+++ b/R/arguments.R
@@ -81,7 +81,8 @@ set_args.model_spec <- function(object, ...) {
     user_specified_mode = object$user_specified_mode,
     method = NULL,
     engine = object$engine,
-    user_specified_engine = object$user_specified_engine
+    user_specified_engine = object$user_specified_engine,
+    quantile_levels = object$quantile_levels
   )
 }
 
diff --git a/R/engines.R b/R/engines.R
index 906a09ccc..ba9ade978 100644
--- a/R/engines.R
+++ b/R/engines.R
@@ -145,7 +145,8 @@ set_engine.model_spec <- function(object, engine, ...) {
     user_specified_mode = object$user_specified_mode,
     method = NULL,
     engine = object$engine,
-    user_specified_engine = TRUE
+    user_specified_engine = TRUE,
+    quantile_levels = object$quantile_levels
   )
 }
 
diff --git a/R/misc.R b/R/misc.R
index 1d79ec16e..40418ba40 100644
--- a/R/misc.R
+++ b/R/misc.R
@@ -374,7 +374,8 @@ new_model_spec <- function(
   user_specified_mode = TRUE,
   method,
   engine,
-  user_specified_engine = TRUE
+  user_specified_engine = TRUE,
+  quantile_levels = NULL
 ) {
   # determine if the model specification could feasibly match any entry
   # in the union of the parsnip model environment and model_info_table.
@@ -386,7 +387,8 @@ new_model_spec <- function(
     user_specified_mode = user_specified_mode,
     method = method,
     engine = engine,
-    user_specified_engine = user_specified_engine
+    user_specified_engine = user_specified_engine,
+    quantile_levels = quantile_levels
   )
   class(out) <- make_classes(cls)
 
diff --git a/R/update.R b/R/update.R
index 9760ded0e..fbdf6ea71 100644
--- a/R/update.R
+++ b/R/update.R
@@ -100,6 +100,7 @@ update_spec <- function(
     user_specified_mode = object$user_specified_mode,
     method = NULL,
     engine = object$engine,
-    user_specified_engine = object$user_specified_engine
+    user_specified_engine = object$user_specified_engine,
+    quantile_levels = object$quantile_levels
   )
 }
diff --git a/man/add_on_exports.Rd b/man/add_on_exports.Rd
index 09c3434fb..75a8eeddc 100644
--- a/man/add_on_exports.Rd
+++ b/man/add_on_exports.Rd
@@ -31,7 +31,8 @@ new_model_spec(
   user_specified_mode = TRUE,
   method,
   engine,
-  user_specified_engine = TRUE
+  user_specified_engine = TRUE,
+  quantile_levels = NULL
 )
 
 check_final_param(x, call = rlang::caller_env())
test_patch:
diff --git a/tests/testthat/test-model_basics.R b/tests/testthat/test-model_basics.R
index 6138e8477..076c2663f 100644
--- a/tests/testthat/test-model_basics.R
+++ b/tests/testthat/test-model_basics.R
@@ -98,3 +98,39 @@ test_that('basic object classes and print methods', {
   expect_true(inherits(svm_rbf(engine = 'kernlab'), 'svm_rbf'))
   expect_true(inherits(svm_rbf(engine = 'liquidSVM'), 'svm_rbf'))
 })
+
+
+test_that('retaining quantile levels', {
+  lvls <- (1:3) / 4
+
+  rf_spec_1 <- rand_forest()
+  expect_null(rf_spec_1$quantile_levels)
+
+  rf_spec_2m <-
+    rf_spec_1 |>
+    set_mode("quantile regression", quantile_levels = lvls)
+
+  expect_equal(rf_spec_2m$quantile_levels, lvls)
+
+  rf_spec_2e <-
+    rf_spec_1 |>
+    set_engine("grf")
+
+  expect_null(rf_spec_2e$quantile_levels)
+
+  rf_spec_2me <-
+    rf_spec_2m |>
+    set_engine("grf")
+
+  expect_equal(rf_spec_2me$quantile_levels, lvls)
+
+  rf_spec_2em <-
+    rf_spec_2e |>
+    set_mode("quantile regression", quantile_levels = lvls)
+
+  expect_equal(rf_spec_2em$quantile_levels, lvls)
+
+  rf_spec_up <- update(rf_spec_2em, parameters = list(mtry = 10))
+
+  expect_equal(rf_spec_up$quantile_levels, lvls)
+})

fail_to_pass: quantile levels are retained when set_mode() is called before set_engine()
quantile levels are retained when set_engine() is called before set_mode()
quantile levels are retained when update() is called on a spec with quantile levels
quantile levels remain NULL when not set
pass_to_pass: basic model spec creation works correctly
set_engine() works correctly for models without quantile levels
set_mode() works correctly for modes other than quantile regression

key_changes: Add `quantile_levels = object$quantile_levels` parameter to the new_model_spec() call in set_args.model_spec() (R/arguments.R)
Add `quantile_levels = object$quantile_levels` parameter to the new_model_spec() call in set_engine.model_spec() (R/engines.R)
Add `quantile_levels = object$quantile_levels` parameter to the new_model_spec() call in update_spec() (R/update.R)
Add `quantile_levels = NULL` as a default parameter to new_model_spec() function definition (R/misc.R)
potential_pitfalls: Forgetting to update all locations where new_model_spec() is called
Not handling the NULL case correctly (when quantile_levels haven't been set)
Missing the update_spec() function which also needs the fix

instruction: Fix the bug where quantile levels are dropped when modifying a model specification. When a user sets quantile levels via `set_mode('quantile regression', quantile_levels = ...)`, these levels should be retained when subsequently calling `set_engine()` or other model spec modification functions. Currently, the order of operations matters: `set_mode() |> set_engine()` loses quantile levels, while `set_engine() |> set_mode()` preserves them. The fix requires ensuring that `quantile_levels` is passed through when creating new model spec objects in the constructor functions.
context:
The parsnip package uses a model specification object that contains various fields including `quantile_levels`. When functions like `set_engine()`, `set_args()`, and `update()` modify a spec, they create a new spec object via `new_model_spec()`. The bug occurs because `quantile_levels` is not being passed from the original object to the new one.

Key function signature from R/misc.R:
r
new_model_spec <- function(
  ...,
  user_specified_mode = TRUE,
  method,
  engine,
  user_specified_engine = TRUE,
  quantile_levels = NULL
)


The fix involves adding `quantile_levels = object$quantile_levels` to the constructor calls in `set_args.model_spec()`, `set_engine.model_spec()`, and `update_spec()`.
